textPlain: Are there any plans for supporting other databases? Our company primarily uses and has experience managing MySQL deployments. I evaluated Temporal some time ago as it seemed like a good fit for what we're building so I'm watching this closely. Thanks!

We are increasingly using Temporal with Temporal Cloud and soon Nexus to manage numerous workflows. I'm curious what type of observability is avialable for DBOS and how much of that you get for "free". The reason we ended up in Temporal was not that previous job-systems were unreliable, it was simply that nobody wanted to go dig through a database to find out what happened with their job, and nobody has time/energy to build a UI just for that purpose.
Hey! Not a DBOS Java question but stumbled on this and looking into it for the python client. Wondering what the support looks like for integration w/ gevent?
Looks great, shame that due to annotation-based API it's gonna be a pain to use in Clojure.

Thanks! On the Python side, we already support SQLite out of the box. Supporting additional databases is feasible from an engineering standpoint, because the core is just SQ,L and we're likely to add more backends over time.Right now, we're focusing on Postgres because it's the most widely adopted database in our user base, and as a startup we need to stay focused. But architecturally, nothing is preventing us from supporting more engines going forward.

There's an observability and workflow management UI: https://docs.dbos.dev/java/tutorials/workflow-managementYou can view your workflows and queues, search/filter them by any number of criteria, visualize graphs of workflow steps, cancel workflows, resume workflows, restart workflows from a specific step--everything you'd want.Currently, this is available as a cloud offering (Conductor), but we're also releasing a self-hostable version of it soon.

gevent works with DBOS Python out of the box (sync DBOS uses Python threading APIs and psycopg3 that gevent monkeypatches).Have you run into any issues using DBOS Python with gevent?  Please let us know!
Upvote on this! Currently building out a project using Flask and gevent, and would love to use DBOS python with my Flask gevent project
